prompt: This code throws ClassCastException at runtime. What is the bug?
choices: Animal must implement Serializable to be cast
a holds a Cat object; casting it to Dog fails at runtime because Cat is not a Dog
Downcasting from Animal to Dog requires a static method
The cast should be (Animal) a, not (Dog) a
explanation: a references a Cat object. Casting to Dog is a downcast. At runtime, Java checks the actual object type. Since Cat is not a subtype of Dog, ClassCastException is thrown. Fix: check with instanceof before casting: if (a instanceof Dog d) { ... }
